mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.disease_name: Ehlers Danlos Syndrome Type IV (EDS IV)
mistralai/Mistral-7B-Instruct-v0.2.matched_phenotypes: joint hypermobility, skin fragility, easy bruising, thin translucent skin, velvety texture, atrophie blanche, ecchymoses, purpura, hernias, varicosities, hemorrhages, epistaxis, rectus diverticulum, mitral valve prolapse, Marfanoid habitus, kyphoscoliosis, flat feet, pes planovalgus, genu valgum, hip dislocation, patella alta, osteopenia, dental problems, periodontitis, tooth decay, delayed dentition, enlargement of cranial sutures, elongated metacarpals, blue or white sclerae, cardiovascular issues, intestinal complications, uterine rupture during pregnancy, chronic pain, Raynaud's phenomenon, orthostatic intolerance, dysautonomia, gastrointestinal symptoms, depression, anxiety, panic attacks, migraines, irritable bowel syndrome, food sensitivities, environmental allergies, asthma, eczema

corneal cloudiness, short stature, coarse facies, lumbar lordosis, skeletal changes, odontoid dysplasia, narrow pelvis, large tongue, respiratory distress, sleep apnea, obstructive airway disorders, cardiac involvement, conductive hearing loss, sensorineural hearing loss, intellectual disability, behavioral disturbances, mild to moderate motor deficit, joint laxity, contractures, scoliosis, chest wall deformities, spine curvature, clubfoot, brachydactylia, short fingers, small hands, short neck, thickened calvarium, prominent forehead, broad nose, full lips, short palpebral fissures, myopia, cataracts, keratoconjunctivitis, dry eyes, recurrent otitis media, frequent ear infections, sinuses, tonsillitis, bronchiectasis, pulmonary function decline, tracheomalacia, wheezing, cough, cyanotic episodes, aspiration pneumonia, constipation, urinary tract infection, renal insufficiency, umbilical hernia, inguinal hernia, hydrocele, testicular atrophy, gynecological manifestations, menstrual cycle alterations, infertility, premature aging, progressive neurological deterioration, muscle weakness, tremors, peripheral neuropathy, autonomic nervous system dysfunction, hearing loss, vision loss, cardiomyopathy, arrhythmias, conduction blocks, congestive heart failure, sudden death

marfanoid body proportions, mental retardation, seizures, characteristic facial features including downslanting palpebral fissures, tented upper lip, micrognathia, long philtrum, spade-shaped nails, ocular signs such as lens dislocations, nystagmus, strabismus, and ectropion; skeletal findings include osteoporosis, scoliosis, kyphosis, and fractures; vascular complications can lead to stroke, deep vein thrombosis, and embolisms; homocystinuria may also cause kidney damage leading to end stage renal disease; other potential health concerns include poor wound healing, decreased fertility, and increased risk of certain types of cancer

angiokeratomas, acroparesthesias, burning pain in extremities, tinnitus, vertigo, hearing loss, corneal opacifications, protein deposits in various organs, especially the heart, kidneys, and brain, resulting in cardiac, renal, and central nervous system involvement respectively; patients often experience gastrointestinal symptoms such as bloating, diarrhea, and abdominal pain

splenomegaly, hepatomegaly, liver cirrhosis, lung involvement with interstitial pulmonary fibrosis, cherry red spot, psychomotor regression, cognitive decline, vertical supranuclear gaze paresis, horizontal gaze palsy, ptosis, external ophthalmoplegia, dysarthria, dysphasia, ataxia, dystonia, rigidity, pyramidal signsd, seizures, growth retardation, short stature, normal intelligence early but later declines, cardiopulmonary compromise, ascites, pleural effusions, edema, jaundice, darkening of the skin, gallbladder stones, xanthomata, xanthelasma, eruptive cutaneous lesions, fatty accumulation around the eye area, and in the perianal region

benign tumors called tubers that develop primarily in the cortex of the brain and sometimes in other areas of the body, including the heart, lungs, kidneys, and skin; these tumors can affect many systems and functions within the body, causing a wide range of symptoms depending upon their location and size; common symptoms include seizures, intellectual disability, autistic behaviors, skin abnormalities known as ash leaf spots, shagreen patches, and facial angiomas, along with noncutaneous findings such as retinal hamartomas, cardiac rhabdomyomas, renal angiomyolipomas, and hepatic angiomyolipomas

mucopolysaccharidosis caused by a deficiency of iduronate sulfatase, which results in the buildup of specific complex sugars called glycosaminoglycans in all bodily fluids and tissues; typical physical characteristics include coarse face, thick tongue, prominent ears, short stature, stiff joints, herniated abdomen, and enlarged head; severe cases present before age two with significant developmental delays, feeding difficulties, breathing problems, and heart conditions; less severe forms might have some residual intellect and mobility
